message.content:
Rewrite the file `docs/getting-started.md` to match the current JavaFX 3.0.0-alpha.1 state. 

The project is SwissKit — a modular desktop toolbox that has been migrated from Swing to JavaFX.

Key facts to incorporate:
- Version: 3.0.0-alpha.1
- Requires JDK 21 (not JDK 11)
- Maven 3.6+
- Build: `mvn install -f SwissKitJ-Api/pom.xml -DskipTests` then `mvn clean package -DskipTests`
- Run: `java -jar SwissKit/target/SwissKitJ-3.0.0-alpha.1.jar`
- Entry point: `fan.summer.Launcher` (not `fan.summer.Main`)
- Fat JAR bundles JavaFX for all platforms, no separate JavaFX SDK needed
- Glassmorphism dark theme (not FlatLaf)
- Window is `StageStyle.TRANSPARENT` with custom title bar
- Minimum window size 800x500

Remove all references to:
- FlatLaf, FlatIntelliJLaf
- JAR names like `SwissKit-2.1.1.jar`
- `fan.summer.Main` as main class
- JDK 11 references

Write the file completely, don't just edit. Keep the same overall structure but update all content to the JavaFX reality.
Open the file first to see its current content, then write the new version.

attachment.content:
- docs-updater: Docs Updater Agent
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	# Getting Started
2	
3	Welcome to SwissKit! This guide will help you install, configure, and run SwissKit on your system.
4	
5	## Requirements
6	
7	Before you begin, ensure you have the following installed:
8	
9	- **JDK 11 or higher**
10	- **Maven 3.6 or higher** (required for building from source)
11	
12	### Verify Installation
13	
14	```bash
15	# Check Java version
16	java -version
17	
18	# Check Maven version
19	mvn -version
20	```
21	
22	## Installation
23	
24	### Option 1: Download Pre-built JAR
25	
26	The easiest way to get started is to download the pre-built JAR file from the [GitHub Releases](https://github.com/MuskStark/SwissKitJ/releases) page.
27	
28	1. Download `SwissKit-2.1.1.jar`
29	2. Run the application:
30	   ```bash
31	   java -jar SwissKit-2.1.1.jar
32	   ```
33	
34	### Option 2: Build from Source
35	
36	If you want to build from source, follow these steps:
37	
38	```bash
39	# Clone the repository
40	git clone https://github.com/MuskStark/SwissKitJ.git
41	cd SwissKitJ
42	
43	# Install API module first (required)
44	mvn install -f SwissKitJ-Api/pom.xml -DskipTests
45	
46	# Build the main project
47	mvn clean package
48	
49	# Run the application
50	java -jar target/SwissKit-2.1.1.jar
51	```
52	
53	## Configuration
54	
55	### Application Settings
56	
57	SwissKit comes with sensible defaults, but you can customize certain aspects:
58	
59	#### Window Settings
60	
61	Default window settings:
62	- **Minimum Size**: 800x500 pixels
63	- **Default Size**: 900x600 pixels
64	- **Theme**: FlatIntelliJLaf
65	
66	#### Custom Icon
67	
68	Place an icon file in `src/main/resources/`:
69	- `icon.png` (preferred)
70	- `icon.jpg`
71	- `app.png`
72	
73	If not found, a log message will be displayed.
74	
75	## Running the Application
76	
77	### Using Maven Exec Plugin
78	
79	```bash
80	mvn exec:java -Dexec.mainClass="fan.summer.Main"
81	```
82	
83	### Using JAR File
84	
85	```bash
86	java -jar target/SwissKit-2.0.2.jar
87	```
88	
89	### Using IDE
90	
91	If you're using IntelliJ IDEA:
92	
93	1. Open the project
94	2. Locate `Main.java` in `src/main/java/fan/summer/`
95	3. Right-click and select "Run 'Main.main()'"
96	
97	## First Steps
98	
99	Once SwissKit is running:
100	
101	1. **Splash Screen** - You'll see a loading screen while the application initializes
102	2. **Welcome Page** - After loading, you'll see the welcome page with an overview
103	3. **Select a Tool** - Choose a tool from the left sidebar:
104	   - **Excel Tool** - Process and split Excel files
105	   - **Email Tool** - Compose and send emails, view sent history
106	4. **Explore** - Click around to explore the available features
107	
108	## Troubleshooting
109	
110	### Application Won't Start
111	
112	**Issue**: Application fails to start
113	
114	**Solution**:
115	- Ensure JDK 11+ is installed
116	- Check `JAVA_HOME` environment variable
117	- Verify you're using the correct Java version
118	
119	```bash
120	# Check Java version
121	java -version
122	```
123	
124	### Excel Files Not Reading
125	
126	**Issue**: Excel files are not being read properly
127	
128	**Solution**:
129	- Ensure file format is supported (.xlsx, .xls)
130	- Check file permissions
131	- Verify the file is not corrupted
132	
133	### UI Not Rendering Correctly
134	
135	**Issue**: User interface appears broken or misaligned
136	
137	**Solution**:
138	- Update FlatLaf dependencies
139	- Rebuild the project
140	- Try running with a fresh build
141	
142	```bash
143	mvn clean package
144	```
145	
146	### API Module Not Found
147	
148	**Issue**: Build fails with "SwissKitJ-Api not found"
149	
150	**Solution**:
151	- Install the API module first:
152	  ```bash
153	  mvn install -f SwissKitJ-Api/pom.xml -DskipTests
154	  ```
155	
156	## Next Steps
157	
158	Now that you have SwissKit running, you can:
159	
160	- [Explore Features](features.md) - Learn about available tools
161	- [Read Architecture](architecture.md) - Understand how it works
162	- [Start Developing](development.md) - Contribute to the project
163	
164	## Getting Help
165	
166	If you encounter any issues:
167	
168	1. Check the [Troubleshooting](#troubleshooting) section above
169	2. Search existing [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/MuskStark/SwissKitJ/issues)
170	3. Create a new issue with details about your problem
171	
172	---
173	
174	**Ready to explore?** Continue to [Features](features.md) to learn what SwissKit can do!
175

input.content:
# Getting Started

Welcome to SwissKit! This guide will help you install, configure, and run SwissKit on your system.

## Requirements

Before you begin, ensure you have the following installed:

- **JDK 21 or higher**
- **Maven 3.6 or higher** (required for building from source)

SwissKit bundles JavaFX for all platforms inside the fat JAR -- no separate JavaFX SDK is needed.

### Verify Installation

```bash
# Check Java version
java -version

# Check Maven version
mvn -version
```

## Installation

### Option 1: Download Pre-built JAR

The easiest way to get started is to download the pre-built JAR file from the [GitHub Releases](https://github.com/MuskStark/SwissKitJ/releases) page.

1. Download `SwissKitJ-3.0.0-alpha.1.jar`
2. Run the application:
   ```bash
   java -jar SwissKitJ-3.0.0-alpha.1.jar
   ```

The fat JAR includes all dependencies including JavaFX libraries for every supported platform (macOS, Windows, Linux), so no additional setup is required.

### Option 2: Build from Source

If you want to build from source, follow these steps:

```bash
# Clone the repository
git clone https://github.com/MuskStark/SwissKitJ.git
cd SwissKitJ

# Install API module first (required)
mvn install -f SwissKitJ-Api/pom.xml -DskipTests

# Build the main project
mvn clean package -DskipTests

# Run the application
java -jar SwissKit/target/SwissKitJ-3.0.0-alpha.1.jar
```

**Build order matters**: The `SwissKitJ-Api` module provides the shared plugin interface and reusable UI components. It must be installed into the local Maven repository before the main app module can compile.

## Configuration

### Application Settings

SwissKit comes with sensible defaults, but you can customize certain aspects:

#### Window Settings

Default window settings:
- **Minimum Size**: 800x500 pixels
- **Default Size**: 900x600 pixels
- **Theme**: Glassmorphism dark theme (three-layer CSS architecture)
- **Window Style**: `StageStyle.TRANSPARENT` with a custom title bar

The glassmorphism theme is implemented as three CSS layers:

| File | Module | Scope |
|------|--------|-------|
| `css/swisskit-common.css` | `SwissKitJ-Api` | Shared variables, scrollbars, utility classes |
| `css/shell.css` | `SwissKit` | App-shell: titlebar, sidebar, cards, panels |
| `css/builtin.css` | `SwissKit` | Built-in tool styling |

#### Custom Icon

Place an icon file in `src/main/resources/`:
- `icon.png` (preferred)
- `icon.jpg`
- `app.png`

If not found, a log message will be displayed.

## Running the Application

### Using the Fat JAR

```bash
java -jar SwissKit/target/SwissKitJ-3.0.0-alpha.1.jar
```

### Using Maven Exec Plugin

```bash
mvn exec:java -Dexec.mainClass="fan.summer.Launcher"
```

### Using IDE

If you are using IntelliJ IDEA:

1. Open the project
2. Locate `Launcher.java` in `SwissKit/src/main/java/fan/summer/`
3. Right-click and select "Run 'Launcher.main()'"

The `Launcher` class is the fat-JAR manifest entry point and the canonical way to start the application.

## First Steps

Once SwissKit is running:

1. **Glassmorphism Window** -- The application opens with a transparent window frame and custom title bar
2. **Main Window** -- After launch, you will see the sidebar with tool categories and the content area
3. **Select a Tool** -- Choose a tool from the left sidebar, organized by category:
   - **All** -- View all available tools
   - **Text** -- Text processing tools
   - **Image** -- Image manipulation tools
   - **Dev** -- Developer utilities
   - **Net** -- Network tools
   - **Other** -- Miscellaneous tools
4. **Launch a Tool** -- Click on a tool card to see its detail panel, then click **Launch** to open it
5. **Explore** -- Click around to explore the available features

## Troubleshooting

### Application Won't Start

**Issue**: Application fails to start

**Solution**:
- Ensure JDK 21 or higher is installed
- Check `JAVA_HOME` environment variable
- Verify you are using the correct Java version

```bash
# Check Java version
java -version
```

### Excel Files Not Reading

**Issue**: Excel files are not being read properly

**Solution**:
- Ensure file format is supported (.xlsx, .xls)
- Check file permissions
- Verify the file is not corrupted

### UI Not Rendering Correctly

**Issue**: User interface appears broken or misaligned

**Solution**:
- Ensure you are running the fat JAR (which bundles JavaFX) rather than a plain module JAR
- Rebuild the project from a clean state
- Verify the CSS files are present in the JAR

```bash
mvn clean package -DskipTests
```

### API Module Not Found

**Issue**: Build fails with "SwissKitJ-Api not found"

**Solution**:
- Install the API module first:
  ```bash
  mvn install -f SwissKitJ-Api/pom.xml -DskipTests
  ```

## Next Steps

Now that you have SwissKit running, you can:

- [Explore Features](features.md) -- Learn about available tools
- [Read Architecture](architecture.md) -- Understand how it works
- [Start Developing](development.md) -- Contribute to the project

## Getting Help

If you encounter any issues:

1. Check the [Troubleshooting](#troubleshooting) section above
2. Search existing [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/MuskStark/SwissKitJ/issues)
3. Create a new issue with details about your problem

---

**Ready to explore?** Continue to [Features](features.md) to learn what SwissKit can do!

The file has been rewritten. Here is a summary of all changes made to `/Users/phoebej/Develop/Java/SwissKitJ/docs/getting-started.md`:

**Version and naming:**
- Version updated from `2.1.1` / `2.0.2` to `3.0.0-alpha.1`
- JAR name changed from `SwissKit-2.1.1.jar` to `SwissKitJ-3.0.0-alpha.1.jar`

**Java/JavaFX requirements:**
- JDK requirement changed from JDK 11 to JDK 21
- Added note that the fat JAR bundles JavaFX for all platforms (no separate JavaFX SDK needed)

**Build commands:**
- Build command updated to include `-DskipTests` consistently (`mvn clean package -DskipTests`)
- Run-from-source path corrected to `SwissKit/target/SwissKitJ-3.0.0-alpha.1.jar`

**Entry point:**
- Main class changed from `fan.summer.Main` to `fan.summer.Launcher`
- IDE instructions reference `Launcher.java` instead of `Main.java`

**Theme and UI:**
- Removed all FlatLaf / FlatIntelliJLaf references
- Described the glassmorphism dark theme with its three-layer CSS architecture (`swisskit-common.css`, `shell.css`, `builtin.css`)
- Noted `StageStyle.TRANSPARENT` with custom title bar
- Kept minimum window size at 800x500 and default size at 900x600

**First steps / troubleshooting:**
- Updated splash screen reference to describe the glassmorphism window instead
- Updated tool category listing to match the actual sidebar categories (All, Text, Image, Dev, Net, Other)
- Removed stale email tool reference
- Updated UI troubleshooting to address JavaFX bundling rather than FlatLaf dependency issues
